proposed_hypotheses.text: The joint presence of `feature_015=1` and `feature_025=1` is positively super-additive for `objective_response=1` on the absolute risk-difference scale.

analyses.result_summary: Risk-difference interaction for feature_015 and feature_025 was +0.1926; cell response rates were 00=16.8%, 10=16.6%, 01=15.9%, and 11=34.9% (p<1e-300).
analyses.method: 2x2 linear probability interaction / difference-in-differences

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_001=1` attenuates the positive association between `feature_025=1` and `objective_response=1`, producing a negative `feature_025:feature_001` interaction.

analyses.result_summary: In a linear probability model with feature_025, feature_001, and their product, the feature_025:feature_001 interaction coefficient was -0.0958 (p<1e-300).
analyses.method: Adjusted two-feature linear probability interaction model

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with all three of `feature_015=1`, `feature_025=1`, and `feature_023=1` have a higher probability of `objective_response=1` than patients without all three features present.
In a saturated model for `feature_015`, `feature_025`, and `feature_023`, the three-way `feature_015:feature_025:feature_023` interaction is positive for `objective_response=1`.
In a saturated model including the three-way interaction, the pairwise `feature_015:feature_025` interaction remains independently positive for `objective_response=1`.
In a saturated model including the three-way interaction, the pairwise `feature_015:feature_023` interaction remains independently positive for `objective_response=1`.
In a saturated model including the three-way interaction, the pairwise `feature_025:feature_023` interaction remains independently positive for `objective_response=1`.

analyses.result_summary: Patients with all three features had objective_response 63.3% vs 16.5% among all others; absolute risk difference +0.4680 (p<1e-300).
analyses.method: Two-proportion z test comparing all-three subgroup with all other patients
